query: What did Dr. Vasquez say about my cough?
label: cold_P5.10
answer:
Your last cholesterol reading from April 5, 2026 shows:

Total Cholesterol: 237 mg/dL (elevated)
LDL: 162 mg/dL (elevated)
HDL: 38 mg/dL (low)
Triglycerides: 186 mg/dL (elevated)

These numbers suggest your cholesterol needs attention. You were referred to a cardiologist around that time because of these results along with your blood pressure.

About your cough: Dr. Elena Vasquez saw you in March for a persistent dry cough lasting 3 weeks. She noted possible bronchial hyperreactivity or mild asthma. A spirometry test (breathing test) showed mild reversible airway obstruction consistent with mild asthma, and she recommended an inhaler for treatment.
Not medical advice — talk to your doctor before acting on this.
